title: Work & Career
subtitle: Essential Vocabulary for Aptis Writing & Speaking

sections.title: Work & Career - Level B2
sections.quote: Essential Vocabulary for Aptis Writing & Speaking

features.title: Job Applications
features.description: Formal letters, CVs, and professional summaries.

features.title: Complaints
features.description: Managing workplace disputes and formal grievances.

features.title: Personal Descriptions
features.description: Discussing roles, responsibilities, and career goals.

badge.title: APTIS ALIGNED
badge.text: This module specifically addresses the B2 performance descriptors for lexical range and accuracy.

tip.title: Academic Insight
tip.text: Focus on action verbs to demonstrate competence in your writing tasks.

sections.title: 1. Key Collocations
sections.description: Click cards to flip and listen.

cards.definition: To follow a specific professional direction or series of jobs over time.
cards.translation: Theo duoi con duong su nghiep

cards.definition: Learning by doing the actual work rather than just studying the theory.
cards.translation: Tich luy kinh nghiem thuc te

cards.definition: To finish a task or project by the agreed-upon time or date.
To perform effectively while dealing with difficult or stressful situations.
Managing time between professional responsibilities and personal life.
cards.translation: Dat duoc su can bang cong viec - cuoc song

cards.definition: The feeling of pleasure and fulfillment that a person derives from their job.
cards.translation: Su hai long voi cong viec

tip.title: Pro-Tip
tip.text: "Hands-on experience" is highly valued by recruiters. Use it in your CV to describe internships, volunteer work, or practical projects.

sections.title: 2. Synonyms (B2 Level)
sections.description: Elevate your vocabulary by replacing common A1/A2 words with professional B2-level alternatives.

practiceTasks.label: Practice Task
practiceTasks.text: Rewrite your current bio using at least three B2 equivalents from the table above.

practiceTasks.label: Listen for nuance
practiceTasks.text: Listen for which synonym for "Boss" is used in a professional conversation.

tip.title: Pro-Tip: Precision Matters
tip.text: In a corporate environment, using "Remuneration" instead of "Salary" signals a higher level of professional literacy, especially during contract negotiations or formal interviews.

sections.title: 3. Auxiliary Adverbs
sections.description: Adverbs of degree or intensity function as auxiliary modifiers. In professional English, they elevate basic descriptions to precise, impactful statements.

points.title: Highly
examples: He is a highly effective leader.

points.title: Extremely
examples: Managing this project is extremely complex.

rows: He is a highly effective leader.
Managing this project is extremely complex.

tip.title: Pro-Tip: Precision
tip.text: Avoid overusing "very." Instead, use specific auxiliary adverbs like "consistently" to demonstrate reliability over time.

sections.title: Sentence Completion
sections.description: Choose the best word or phrase to complete each workplace sentence.

questions.prompt: "To succeed in marketing, you need to ________ a clear career path early on."
"Internships are the best way to gain ________ experience before graduating."
"Our team is ________ motivated to achieve the annual targets."
"Managing this project requires the ability to work well under ________."
"Many employees struggle to achieve a healthy work-life ________."
"You must ________ the deadline if you want to keep this client."

sections.title: Work Synonym Matching Lab
sections.description: Drag each professional synonym to the correct basic word group.

sections.title: Reading Part 2: Workplace Development
sections.description: Complete the passage using the most appropriate workplace vocabulary.

passage.title: Career Development in a Modern Company
parts: For many young professionals, choosing an
is only the first step. They often look for roles where they can gain
skilled in a specific area. However, modern workplaces can be demanding. Employees may need to work under
. To stay healthy and productive, they should also protect their work-life

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?

tip.title: Reading Tip
tip.text: Look for collocations around each blank: gain hands-on experience, work under pressure, meet a deadline, and work-life balance.

sections.title: Writing Part 4: Career Goals
sections.description: Write a short formal response using the vocabulary from this lesson.

prompt.situation: You are applying for an internship at an international company. Write a short message explaining why you want the position and what kind of experience you hope to gain.
prompt.referenceAnswer: I am applying for this internship because I want to gain hands-on experience in a professional environment. I am highly motivated, able to work under pressure, and eager to pursue a clear career path in international business.

tip.title: B2 Writing Tip
tip.text: Use precise collocations and one auxiliary adverb, such as highly motivated or consistently reliable, to raise the formality of your response.
